political.s: Linde engages in modest political giving with approximately $68,156 in 2024 PAC contributions and $610,000 in lobbying expenditures. The company maintains bipartisan political engagement focused on industrial policy and regulatory matters affecting its operations.

charity.s: Linde reported $10.4 million in global charitable giving in 2022 focused on STEM education, diversity and inclusion, community support, environment, disaster relief and health initiatives. The company maintains active employee volunteering programs with 14,000+ volunteer engagements in 2023 benefiting approximately 350,000 people globally.
charity.focus: STEM education, diversity and inclusion, community support, environment, disaster relief, health and wellness

environment.s: Linde reported carbon emissions of approximately 25.4 billion kg CO2e in 2023 with a DitchCarbon score of 43/100. The company has committed to net-zero emissions by 2050 for Scopes 1 and 2 but is not committed to specific near-term reduction initiatives. While Linde helps customers reduce emissions by more than double its own footprint, internal emissions remain substantial.

labor.s: Specific OSHA and NLRB violation data for Linde is not readily available in search results. The company employs approximately 65,000 people globally and emphasizes safety through its HSE policy and "Zero Today" safety initiative, though detailed labor practice records are not disclosed in public sources reviewed.

dei.s: Linde was named a Top 50 company by Fair360 in 2024 for diversity, equity and inclusion, demonstrating commitment to workplace fairness. The company also named to Top Companies for Executive Women and Top Companies for Asian American Executives lists, with Inclusion as one of five core values.

animals.s: Linde is committed to animal welfare with a stated goal to eliminate animal testing. Animal testing is only used where required by government regulation or as a last resort after exploring all alternatives including computer models and scientific literature.

guns.s: As an industrial gases company, Linde does not manufacture or sell firearms. The company's business focuses on production and delivery of gases and engineering services.

privacy.s: Not disclosed. Linde does not appear to have published a comprehensive data privacy policy or privacy-related statements in publicly available sources.

execPay.s: CEO Sanjiv Lamba received $20.7 million in total compensation in 2024, with 72-79% of target direct compensation being variable and performance-based. The company maintains mandatory stock ownership requirements (6X base salary for CEO) and clawback provisions for accountability.

sec.note: Pay ratio extracted from proxy filing — verify manually for accuracy
